column1: Low-income economies experienced significant output losses amid elevated borrowing costs compared to their pre-pandemic paths.
Financial conditions in Emerging Markets and Developing Economies (EMDEs) were tight, especially for sovereigns with weaker
creditworthiness, resulting in increased financial strains evidenced by substantial currency depreciations and capital outflows. Growth in
sub-Saharan Africa decelerated to 2.9 percent in 2023, influenced by heightened living expenses, which dampened consumption growth,
alongside an escalation of political instability in certain areas of the region. The region also faced high debt and interest rates, which
constrained fiscal flexibility and amplified financing requirements.
